summary: End-to-end test of Task Drop Day Plinko (Expo web preview + FastAPI backend). All 5 backend pytest tests pass (root, list_assets, generate ball-clock with valid base64 PNG, invalid asset rejection with 400, missing name 400). All frontend flows pass on mobile viewport 420x900: welcome tutorial with 3 slides and 'Plan My Day' CTA, Today tab with placeholder board + Drop button + Add Task, AddTaskSheet modal (input, priority high, submit), task toggle checkbox, Drop screen with risk toggles, ball drop animation, result-format-card + Continue, today-format-card on return, full tab nav (Tomorrow/Stats/Settings), Settings time-picker (Morning Drop -> 09:00 persisted), Stats metrics (day-clear, streak, sprints), Reset flow + tutorial re-shown after full reload. No JS console errors. No React Native red screens. Gemini Nano Banana image generation working (returns valid base64). One important note: backend now returns mime_type 'image/jpeg' (not 'image/png') from Gemini even though the prompt asks for PNG — assertion only checks 'image/' prefix and passes, but worth noting if frontend uses extension assumptions.

minor.issue: Gemini returns 'image/jpeg' mime_type even though prompts request transparent PNG; transparency on PNG will not be achievable. AssetImage.tsx relies on the returned data URL so functionally OK, but cards/badges will have a JPEG background instead of transparent.

action_items: Optional: if transparent backgrounds are important for badges/cards, switch the image generation model variant that supports PNG with alpha, or composite over the dark UI background — current returns JPEG.
Optional: state.ts assumes data is PNG-only when building data URLs in some places; verify AssetImage handles 'image/jpeg' mime as-is (it does, since the API returns mime_type and frontend uses it).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: logger is referenced inside generate_asset (line 119) but defined later at module load (line 144). It's fine in practice because Python resolves at call time, but consider moving logger setup above the route definitions for clarity.
drop.tsx returns to Today via router.back() then router.push('/full-sprint') with a 200ms setTimeout — works but is timing-dependent; consider router.replace to be safer.
AsyncStorage 'reset then router.replace' relies on state subscribers re-evaluating index gate after onboarded=false; verified working end-to-end.
seed_data_creation: None — app is fully local; created one TEST task via UI to exercise flow, removed via reset.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file lives at /app/backend/tests/test_plinko_api.py and runs in ~10s; one of the tests performs a real Gemini call (5-15s). Frontend tests can use viewport 420x900 and need to clear localStorage between runs to retrigger the welcome screen. After tapping reset-button + confirm-reset-button, do a full page.goto() (not page.reload) to verify tutorial re-shows, because expo-router caches the current /(tabs) URL on reload.
